treatments.label: Stepping affected foot on/off block, Increase the need for dorsiflexion by bringing block closer to toes so toes catch if insufficient dorsiflexion
Stepping with affected foot behind intact (start with affected ankle in plantar flexion, stepping into heel strike)
Step with affected foot and place obstacles in front , the closer it is , the harder it will be
Walking emphasising walking with heel strike at initial contact.
Walking on a treadmill set at a slight incline to demand more DF
Walking over obstacles
Walking over grass, carpet or other surfaces to increase DF
Stepping affected foot on/off block. Increase the need for hip flexion by bringing block closer to toes so foot will catch if insufficient hip flexion
stepping with affected foot behind intact (start with affected hip in hip extension, stepping into heel strike)
Walking emphasising walking with knees up to encoruage hip flexion
Hip flexion wth nonaffected leg standing on block with so that affected foot doesn’t touch the ground
Swing phase, for slow walkers specifically train knee flexion at beginning of swing
Active training into a lengthened ROM
Ankle dorsiflexion to target in sitting
Ankle dorsiflexion in side lying / gravity eliminated position
Ankle dorsiflexion with electrical stimulation
Manual assisted hip flexion with gravity eliminated / with assisted support , e sstim is not feasible
Hip flexion in gravity eliminated position without manual assistance/ to minimise friction
Progressive resistance training for hip flexion with thera band in sitting / standing
E stim for knee flexors
Knee flexors in gravity eliminated position without manual assistance/ to minimise friction
Progressive resistance training for knee flexion with thera band in sitting / standing
Improve ability to decelerate hip flexion by controlling hip flexion on prone, or anything that will target control of hip flexion
Improve ability to decelerate knee extension by controlling knee extension on prone, or anything that will target control of knee extension
Rapidly dorsiflex in a static position ( sitting / standing )
Practise Hip flexion facing a fall to force knee flexion as insufficient knee flexion will prevent patient from performing a successful hip flexion with knees touching wall

Practise tasks that require patient to eccentrically control hip flexion and knee extension in a standing position, e.g use verbal/ auditory cues to prompt controlled knee extension / hip flexion to target

Interventions that may or may not address sensory / proprioception impairments ( mirror therapy, exposing limb to different textures ) noted there is unsifficient evidence to be used routinely in a clinical setting

Interventions to attempt to lengthen knee flexors using the following principles, positioning, sustained stretching, loading in a lengthened position, note that current evidence do not clearly support interventions to increase muscle length
Gastrocs strengthening in standing on a reverse wedge with back against wall and lower heels down to floor
Strengthening/part practice: back against wall, move body forwards away from wall by dorsiflexing at ankles – increase body weight on affected leg (eg, scales to monitor)
Plantarflexor strengthening in lying against resistance (wall, end of bed, tilt-table preferably with feedback eg scales, pressure monitor)
Part practice: plantarflexing in standing, verbal cue to emphasise eccentric control and to switch between eccentric control and rapid concentric activity.
Electrical stimulation to increase strength of hip extension
Hip extension strengthening in supine with affected leg over edge of bed
Electrical stimulation to increase strength of knee extensors
Knee extension strengthening with ankle weight, therabands, leg press or sliding tilt table (apply principles of progressive resistance training)
Knee extension strengthening in gravity eliminated position / minimise friction
Strengthening/part practice: step up onto block with affected leg (forwards or sideways)

kinematic_deviations.label: Early and mid swing - Decreased peak knee flexion
Decreased knee extension prior to heel strike
Knee hyperextension at end of swing prior to heel strike

impairments.impairment: Decreased strength ankle dorsiflexors (concentric)
impairments.testing: MMT ankle dorsiflexors sitting, knee in flexion

impairments.impairment: Decreased ability to rapidly dorsiflex when knees are extended / flexed
impairments.testing: Test ability to dorsiflex rapidly and repetitively

impairments.impairment: Decreased ability to maintain DF with knee extension
impairments.testing: Test ability to maintain DF whilst flexing and extending knee

impairments.impairment: Decreased sensation in ankle
impairments.testing: Subjective perception of sensation. If loss suspected then complete a proprioception test / sensation test, eg, Nottingham

impairments.impairment: Decreased passive ankles dorsiflexion (contracture, spasticity ankle plantarflexors)
Decreased strength hip flexors (concentric)
impairments.testing: MMT – test iliopsoas, not quadriceps e.g. knee in flexion

impairments.impairment: Decreased ability to rapidly flex hip
impairments.testing: Test ability to flex hip rapidly

impairments.impairment: Decreased sensation in the hip
Decreased strength in knee flexors in slow walker (concentric)
Decreased ability to have selective hip flexion ( without knee extension)
impairments.testing: Test ability to flex hip rapidly with knee flexion

impairments.impairment: Decreased strength in hip extensors to decelerate hip flexion (isometric/eccentric)
Decreased strength in knee flexors to decelerate knee extension (isometric/eccentric)
Decreased ability to coordinate eccentric activity of knee flexor / hip extensor to control  hip flexion and knee extension prior to heel strike
impairments.testing: Test ability to control hip flex hip with knee extension

impairments.impairment: Decreased strength in ankle plantarflexors (isometric/eccentric)
Decreased strength hip extensors (concentric, early stance)
Decreased strength knee extensors (concentric)
Inability to produce sufficient active tension with hip flexor in pre swing or early swing
Inability to produce sufficient active tension with knee flexor muscles in pre swing
Increased active tension of knee flexors muscle in pre swing
impairments.testing: EMG recording in stepping/completing a foot tap

impairments.impairment: Decreased passive knee extension (contracture, spasticity knee flexor)
Increased active tension of plantarflexors muscles in pre swing
impairments.testing: EMG recording in stepping/completing a foot tap

impairments.impairment: Inability to produce sufficient active tension with knee extensor muscles in early swing
Inability to produce active tension with the dorsiflexor muscles in swing
Decreased strength hip flexors (eccentric, mid to late stance)
Inability to coordinate leg extension with controlled weight shift onto stance leg
impairments.testing: Assess ability to maintain knee and hip extension whilst shifting weight on the leg and stepping

impairments.impairment: Decreased passive hip extension (contracture, spasticity hip flexors)
Increased active tension of hip flexor musles in stance
impairments.testing: EMG recording in stepping/completing a foot tap

impairments.impairment: Increased active tension of ankle plantarflexor musles in stance
impairments.testing: EMG recording in stepping/completing a foot tap
